title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Detective Vane confirms the identity of the deceased Mr. Braid by identifying a strawberry-shaped birthmark on his right thigh. While Mrs. Braid maintains that her husband lived a secluded life without enemies, Jonah Lincoln reveals a past conflict involving a man named Paul Roman. Lincoln describes Roman as a fraudulent individual who served as a disciple to a man named Harakat on Oyster Island, where they operated a nudist community. Lincoln notes that both Mr. Braid and a neighbor, Dr. Temple, disapproved of Roman's activities.

The investigation further explores the local community and Braid's connections. Dr. Temple, a retired military doctor, rents property to the east of Braid and was a frequent chess partner of the deceased. To the west, a British couple, Percy and Elizabeth Rein, rent property belonging to the Braid family. Mrs. Braid notes that Thomas was an exceptionally skilled chess player who once played to a draw against a national champion.

Ellery questions the witnesses regarding several names—Varga Krossak, Clay, Andrew Farn, and Alroy—but receives no recognition. He also inquires about the timing of Harakat's arrival on Oyster Island, which Lincoln identifies as March, noting that Paul Roman arrived at the same time. Mrs. Braid reveals that Mr. Braid immigrated to America eighteen years ago with Stephen Megalos, suggesting they were former friends or business partners in Romania. Upon learning that the Braid family was once wealthy, Ellery concludes the questioning by requesting a map book from the district attorney, Mr. Escham.
